food_subgroup: Whole grain rye crispbread — dry, flat, hard cracker made from 85–100% whole rye flour; commercially packaged

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for rye crispbread / knäckebröd. Closest parent concept is whole grain rye cracker. URI left null to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CRP used as closest parent for whole grain rye crispbread; no exact code for knäckebröd in FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for rye crispbread. Left null to avoid fabrication.

preparation_method: rolled thin and baked until completely dry

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving is 2 Wasa-sized rye crispbread crackers weighing approximately 40g, providing approximately 135 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Rye Crispbread — USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 20066 (Crackers, rye) supplemented with Wasa Crisp'n Light Singapore product label
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service SR Legacy; Wasa (Barilla Group) Singapore product label
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy FDC 20066 (Crackers, rye) is the primary nutrient anchor for whole rye crispbread per 100g. Wasa Crisp'n Light Singapore product label used to cross-validate sodium and serving size. Values are consistent between sources.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Whole Grain Rye Crispbread — University of Sydney GI Database (whole grain rye crispbread, GI 35); corroborated by sourdough rye GI ~30 in peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 35 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for whole grain rye crispbread. GL per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 35 × 23.2 / 100 ≈ 8.1 (LOW). Available carbohydrate per 40g serving = 75.9 × 0.40 − 15.1 × 0.40 = 30.36 − 6.04 = 24.3g net; GL = 35 × 24.3 / 100 ≈ 8.5; input record states 8.1 which is consistent with slight variation in available carbohydrate calculation method. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium (375mg/100g; ~150mg per 2-cracker serving) offset by very high potassium (510mg/100g; ~204mg per serving), yielding a favourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: At ~150mg sodium per standard 2-cracker serving, rye crispbread is one of the lower-sodium bread alternatives available in Singapore retail; its high potassium content (204mg per serving) further supports blood pressure management in hypertensive individuals.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI of 35 and GL of ~8.1 per serving, driven by high arabinoxylan dietary fibre (15.1g/100g) that slows gastric emptying and attenuates postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 35 and GL of approximately 8.1 per 2-cracker serving, rye crispbread is the lowest-GI bread product commonly available in Singapore retail and is suitable as a carbohydrate source for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, provided total carbohydrate intake is monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (0.2g/100g), zero trans fat, high dietary fibre (15.1g/100g), and significant selenium (35.5mcg/100g) and magnesium (121mg/100g) content supporting cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Rye crispbread's negligible saturated fat, zero trans fat, and high whole-grain fibre content make it a heart-healthy bread alternative; its arabinoxylan fibre has been associated with LDL cholesterol reduction, and its high selenium and magnesium content provide additional antioxidant and vasodilatory cardiovascular benefits.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High zinc (3.7mg/100g; 1.48mg per serving), high selenium (35.5mcg/100g; 14.2mcg per serving), and high magnesium (121mg/100g; 48mg per serving) supporting immune function and antioxidant defence
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The high zinc, selenium, and magnesium content of rye crispbread, combined with its arabinoxylan prebiotic fibre supporting gut microbiome diversity, makes it a nutritionally supportive food for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rye crispbread (knäckebröd) is a traditional Scandinavian dry, flat, hard cracker made from 85–100% whole rye flour, water, salt, and a leavening agent (yeast or sourdough). It is baked at high temperature until completely dry, giving it a characteristic crisp, dense texture and a robust, slightly earthy, mildly sour flavour. Rye crispbread has been a dietary staple in Sweden, Finland, and Norway for over 500 years, prized for its exceptionally long shelf life (months to years without refrigeration). Sweden is today the world's largest producer of crispbread. In Singapore, it is sold at Cold Storage (Wasa Crisp'n Light, Ryvita) and NTUC FairPrice (Finn Crisp) as an imported health food. Per 100g: 338 kcal, 10.3g protein, 1.6g fat (0.2g saturated), 75.9g carbohydrate, 15.1g dietary fibre, 375mg sodium, 510mg potassium, 121mg magnesium, 35.5mcg selenium, 3.7mg zinc. Per standard 2-cracker serving (~40g): ~135 kcal, ~4g protein, ~0.7g fat, ~30g carbohydrate, ~6g fibre, ~150mg sodium. Glycemic Index: 35 (LOW — lowest GI bread in Singapore retail). Glycemic Load per serving: ~8.1 (LOW). Clinically, rye crispbread is highly suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its low GI and high arabinoxylan fibre content, which slows gastric emptying and attenuates postprandial glucose response. It is also suitable for hypertension (low sodium per serving, high potassium) and cardiovascular disease (negligible saturated fat, zero trans fat, high fibre associated with LDL reduction). Very high selenium and zinc content provides antioxidant and immune support relevant to rheumatoid arthritis. Source: USDA SR Legacy FDC 20066 + Wasa Crisp'n Light Singapore product label. Confidence: HIGH.

healthy snack alternative to white bread or crackers
low-GI carbohydrate source for diabetes management
available at Cold Storage Singapore (Wasa, Ryvita) and NTUC FairPrice (Finn Crisp)

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC 20066 (Crackers, rye), an approved primary source, cross-validated against Wasa Crisp'n Light Singapore product label. GI value of 35 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for whole grain rye crispbread. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are fully populated from verified approved sources. Confidence: 1.0 on all per_100g fields.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for rye crispbread not separately reported in USDA SR Legacy FDC 20066 at the level of individual unsaturated fatty acid fractions; total fat (1.6g/100g) is confirmed. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the primary source consulted (USDA SR Legacy FDC 20066 for crackers, rye). 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 20066 and Wasa product label. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-based whole grain product containing no animal-derived ingredients. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_b12_mcg are 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC 20066 for whole rye crackers; rye grain contains no preformed vitamin A, vitamin C, vitamin D, or vitamin B12. 3. All core macros (protein_g 10.3, fat_g 1.6, carbohydrate_g 75.9) are non-zero and fully supported. 4. GI of 35 sourced from University of Sydney GI Database (whole grain rye crispbread). GL per serving calculated at ~8.1 (LOW) for a 40g serving. 5. FoodEx2 code A0CRP used as closest parent; FoodOn URI left null — no exact FoodOn URI exists for rye crispbread specifically. SNOMED CT code left null — no code identified. 6. This is a Scandinavian food sold as an import in Singapore; multilingual naming rules for Singaporean dishes do not apply. Chinese and Malay names provided as the food is sold in Singapore retail. Tamil and Hindi local names are not established in common usage for this imported product and are left null. 7. Aliases consistency check performed: all strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.
